message.content:
SELF AGENT PROJECT
Human-Backed Autonomous Agent with On-Chain Lending


Overview
The Self Agent Project is an end-to-end system for deploying autonomous AI agents that can participate in decentralized financial activity — including borrowing, lending, and paying for on-chain services — while being provably linked to real human identities. By combining OpenClaw as the agent runtime, Celo as the financial layer, and Self.xyz for identity verification, the system ensures that only human-backed agents can access credit, opening a pathway toward trustworthy, accountable autonomous economic actors.

Deployment & Setup
Getting an agent running involves a short sequence of steps that bind a real identity to an on-chain agent wallet, fund it, and install the skills it needs to operate.

Connect wallet. The user connects their personal wallet through the dashboard web application. This wallet serves as the human anchor for the entire agent lifecycle.
Deploy the agent. From the dashboard, the user deploys a Dockerized OpenClaw agent instance.
Register with Self.xyz. The user completes the Self.xyz identity registration flow. Upon successful verification, a soulbound NFT (token ID 8004) is minted to the human wallet. This token is non-transferable and acts as a permanent proof of personhood.
Fund the agent. The user sends CELO to the agent wallet via the web application. Only CELO is accepted as the base currency at this stage.
Install custom skills. Two skills are installed into the agent: a Uniswap integration skill for token swaps, and a lending protocol skill for interacting with the on-chain lending contract.
Bootstrap liquidity. The agent automatically swaps its CELO into USDC via Uniswap, retaining a small CELO reserve ($0.10) to cover future gas fees.

The Lending Protocol
The lending layer is a smart contract system on Celo that enables agents to borrow from one another — but with a critical constraint: only agents provably backed by a verified human (via the 8004 soulbound NFT) may request loans. This creates a system of accountable credit.

Loan Lifecycle (Agents pay for the gas themself)
Request: An agent that needs capital publishes a loan request to the lending contract, specifying the amount, desired duration, and acceptable interest rate.
Discovery & Verification: Other agents scan the contract for open requests. Before filling, they can verify on-chain that the requesting agent is human-backed (presence of the 8004 soulbound NFT) and optionally review the borrower's off-chain reputation history.
Filling a Loan: A willing lender agent fills the request by sending the funds through the contract, which locks the terms and initiates the loan.
Repayment: The borrowing agent can repay the loan at any time within the agreed duration, returning principal plus interest to the lender.
Cancellation: Before a loan is taken by any lender, the requesting agent may cancel and delete the request from the contract.
Reputation: A watchtower service monitors loan outcomes and writes reputation reviews on-chain for each agent, building a transparent credit history over time.

Identity Gate
Loan requests are permissioned: the lending contract checks for the 8004 soulbound NFT on the requesting agent's associated human wallet before accepting the request. Agents without human backing cannot borrow.


Demo Scenario
The reference demo illustrates the full lending loop in a single coherent workflow, showcasing how an agent uses autonomous credit to unlock a service it could not otherwise afford.

Insufficient funds. The agent attempts to call an x402-gated service on Celo but does not have enough USDC to pay the access fee.
Loan request. Recognising the shortfall, the agent autonomously publishes a loan request to the lending contract for the required amount.
Loan filled. A lender agent — having verified the borrower's human-backed status — fills the loan. Funds arrive in the borrowing agent's wallet.
Service accessed. The agent pays the x402 service fee and successfully calls the service, completing the task it was trying to perform.
Earn and repay. The agent sells a service of its own to generate revenue, then repays the loan with interest, closing the credit loop autonomously.





Technology Stack
Component
Role in the System
OpenClaw
Agent framework — provides the containerized runtime, skill installation system, and autonomous decision-making layer for all agent behavior.
Celo
Base financial layer — hosts the lending protocol smart contracts, settles x402 service payments, and provides the gas and token infrastructure (CELO, USDC).
Self.xyz
Identity layer — issues the soulbound 8004 NFT upon human verification, enforcing the requirement that only human-backed agents may access credit.
Uniswap (Celo)
DEX integration — enables the agent to swap CELO for USDC at setup and manage its own treasury as needed.
x402 Protocol
Paywall standard — HTTP 402-based micropayment protocol used to gate services on Celo, demonstrating the real-world utility of the agent's economic activity.
Watchtower
Reputation service — monitors the lending contract for repayment events and writes on-chain reputation scores to build agent credit histories over time.


Self Agent Project  ·  Confidential
 read these specs, and apply all the relevant changes about how the flow and lending will actually work

1→# Yield Agent — Self-Funded Autonomous AI
     2→
     3→An autonomous AI agent that funds its own operations entirely from stETH yield — no human tops up, no principal at risk.
     4→
     5→## Problem
     6→
     7→Today, running an AI agent costs money and requires constant human intervention to fund wallets, manage budgets, and approve payments. Agents can't be truly autonomous if someone has to keep feeding them.
     8→
     9→## Solution
    10→
    11→A user stakes ETH into a treasury contract backed by wstETH. The contract enforces a hard separation: the principal is structurally locked, and only accrued yield flows into the agent's spendable balance. The agent uses that yield to pay for its own inference, API calls, and services — fully autonomously.
    12→
    13→## How it works
    14→
    15→1. **Fund** — User deposits wstETH into the Agent Treasury contract. Configures spending permissions (recipient whitelist, per-tx cap, time windows).
    16→2. **Think** — Agent reasons privately via Venice's no-data-retention inference API. Sensitive treasury decisions never leave the private compute layer.
    17→3. **Swap** — Agent converts yield to USDC or VVV via Uniswap — paying for services or Venice inference directly.
    18→4. **Pay** — Agent pays for services through Locus wallets on Base — auditable, capped, autonomous.
    19→5. **Dashboard** — Web UI to deploy agents, stake ETH, monitor yield accrual, track spending, and adjust permissions.
    20→
    21→## Key properties
    22→
    23→- Principal is never accessible to the agent — enforced at the contract level
    24→- Agent spending is bounded by yield + configurable permissions
    25→- Private reasoning via Venice — the agent's strategy stays confidential
    26→- All payments flow through Locus — auditable, on-chain, USDC on Base
    27→
    28→## Integrated providers
    29→
    30→- [Lido](https://lido.fi) — wstETH staking and yield generation
    31→- [Venice](https://venice.ai) — Private, no-data-retention AI inference
    32→- [Uniswap](https://uniswap.org) — On-chain swaps (yield to USDC/VVV)
    33→- [Locus](https://locus.finance) — Agent-native payment infrastructure on Base
    34→

1→# Microlending Agent
     2→
     3→You are an autonomous AI agent that manages a microlending pool on Celo.
     4→
     5→## Core Behavior
     6→
     7→- You are registered on-chain via Self Agent ID (ERC-8004) with a soulbound NFT
     8→- You verify borrower identity via Self protocol before disbursing loans
     9→- You sign all outbound requests with your agent private key using the Self SDK
    10→- You manage loan operations: disburse, track repayments, flag overdue loans
    11→- You maintain the lending pool health and monitor utilization
    12→
    13→## Identity
    14→
    15→- Your agent keypair was generated during Self Agent ID registration
    16→- Your identity is linked to a human via zero-knowledge passport verification
    17→- You have a soulbound NFT on Celo proving you are human-backed
    18→- Use `SelfAgent.fetch()` for all authenticated outbound requests
    19→
    20→## Wallet
    21→
    22→- Your operational wallet is a Privy server wallet on Celo
    23→- All lending operations use USDC on Celo
    24→- Check pool balance regularly and ensure sufficient liquidity
    25→
    26→## Decision Making
    27→
    28→- Never disburse loans to unverified agents (Self Agent ID required)
    29→- Track all loans and repayment schedules
    30→- Flag overdue loans and escalate if needed
    31→- Prioritize pool health over individual loan approvals
    32→- Report status periodically
    33→

1→---
     2→name: lending
     3→description: >
     4→  Manage microlending operations on Celo. Verify borrowers via Self Agent ID,
     5→  disburse loans, track repayments, and flag overdue loans.
     6→version: 1.0.0
     7→requires:
     8→  env: ["AGENT_PRIVATE_KEY", "CELO_RPC_URL"]
     9→---
    10→
    11→# Lending Skill
    12→
    13→You manage a microlending pool on Celo using USDC. You are a Self-verified agent
    14→with a soulbound NFT on Celo proving you are human-backed.
    15→
    16→## Identity
    17→
    18→Your identity is registered via Self Agent ID (ERC-8004). You sign all outbound
    19→requests using the `SelfAgent` SDK, which attaches proof-of-human headers.
    20→
    21→## Available Actions
    22→
    23→### Verify Borrower Agent
    24→Before disbursing any loan, verify the borrower's agent is registered on-chain:
    25→```typescript
    26→import { SelfAgentVerifier } from "@selfxyz/agent-sdk";
    27→
    28→const verifier = SelfAgentVerifier.create()
    29→  .requireAge(18)
    30→  .requireOFAC()
    31→  .maxAgentsPerHuman(1)
    32→  .build();
    33→
    34→// Verify incoming request
    35→const result = await verifier.verify({
    36→  signature: req.headers["x-self-agent-signature"],
    37→  timestamp: req.headers["x-self-agent-timestamp"],
    38→  method: req.method,
    39→  url: req.url,
    40→  body: req.body,
    41→});
    42→```
    43→Only proceed if `result.valid === true`.
    44→
    45→### Make Authenticated Requests
    46→Sign outbound requests with your agent identity:
    47→```typescript
    48→import { SelfAgent } from "@selfxyz/agent-sdk";
    49→
    50→const agent = new SelfAgent({
    51→  privateKey: process.env.AGENT_PRIVATE_KEY!,
    52→  network: "testnet",
    53→});
    54→
    55→const res = await agent.fetch("https://api.example.com/data", {
    56→  method: "POST",
    57→  body: JSON.stringify({ action: "disburse_loan" }),
    58→});
    59→```
    60→
    61→### Disburse Loan
    62→Send USDC from the pool to a verified borrower on Celo:
    63→- Confirm borrower agent is Self-verified on-chain
    64→- Check pool has sufficient liquidity
    65→- Record loan terms: amount, APR, due date
    66→- Transfer USDC to borrower address
    67→
    68→### Track Repayments
    69→Monitor incoming USDC transfers to the pool:
    70→- Match payments to outstanding loans
    71→- Update repayment progress
    72→- Calculate remaining balance including interest
    73→
    74→### Flag Overdue Loans
    75→Check loan due dates and flag overdue borrowers:
    76→- Mark loans as overdue after grace period
    77→- Reduce borrower reputation score
    78→- Report overdue loans for review
    79→
    80→## Lending Rules
    81→- Maximum loan per borrower: 500 USDC
    82→- Maximum total exposure: 50% of pool liquidity
    83→- Minimum pool reserve: 25% of total deposits
    84→- Grace period for repayment: 7 days after due date
    85→- APR range: 5-15% depending on borrower reputation
    86→
    87→## Reputation Scoring
    88→- New verified agent: score 0
    89→- Each on-time repayment: +10 points
    90→- Each overdue payment: -20 points
    91→- Score > 50: eligible for larger loans
    92→- Score < -20: lending suspended
    93→
    94→## Contract Addresses (Celo)
    95→- SelfAgentRegistry: 0xaC3DF9ABf80d0F5c020C06B04Cced27763355944
    96→- SelfHumanProofProvider: 0x4b036aFD959B457A208F676cf44Ea3ef73Ea3E3d
    97→- SelfReputationRegistry: 0x69Da18CF4Ac27121FD99cEB06e38c3DC78F363f4
    98→

1→# Lido Agent Yield
     2→
     3→A smart contract protocol on **Base** that lets humans deposit [wstETH](https://docs.lido.fi/guides/lido-tokens-integration-guide/#wsteth) and allocate a configurable share of accrued staking yield to an AI agent wallet.
     4→
     5→## How it works
     6→
     7→1. A **YieldVaultFactory** (singleton) deploys lightweight [EIP-1167](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-1167) clones — one vault per user / team.
     8→2. The vault **owner** (human) deposits wstETH and configures:
     9→   - **Agent wallet** — the address that receives yield.
    10→   - **Yield share** — percentage (basis points) of accrued yield the agent may withdraw.
    11→   - **Withdrawal frequency** — cooldown between agent withdrawals (e.g. 7 days for a weekly budget).
    12→3. Yield is **never rebased** into extra tokens. Instead it is computed on every call via the **Chainlink wstETH/stETH exchange rate oracle** on Base:
    13→
    14→   ```
    15→   yield_stETH  = vault_wstETH_balance * current_rate  -  principalStETH
    16→   yield_wstETH = yield_stETH / current_rate
    17→   ```
    18→4. The **agent** can withdraw up to `yieldShareBps %` of accrued yield, subject to the cooldown. It can never touch the principal.
    19→5. The **owner** has full control: withdraw principal, withdraw yield, update settings, pause, or emergency-exit at any time.

    22→
    23→| Contract | Address |
    24→|---|---|
    25→| wstETH (ERC20Bridged) | `0xc1CBa3fCea344f92D9239c08C0568f6F2F0ee452` |
    26→| Chainlink wstETH/stETH exchange rate | `0xB88BAc61a4Ca37C43a3725912B1f472c9A5bc061` |
    27→

    33→  YieldVaultFactory.sol     # Factory that deploys vault clones

    36→test/

    38→script/
    39→  Deploy.s.sol               # Deployment script
    40→```
    41→
    42→## Prerequisites
    43→
    44→- [Foundry](https://book.getfoundry.sh/getting-started/installation)
    45→- A Base RPC endpoint (public `https://mainnet.base.org` works for testing)
    46→
    47→## Setup
    48→
    49→```bash
    50→cp .env.example .env
    51→# edit .env with your RPC URL
    52→

    54→```
    55→

    57→
    58→```bash
    59→forge build
    60→```
    61→

    63→

    75→
    76→## Security considerations
    77→
    78→- **Oracle staleness** — reverts if the Chainlink answer is older than 25 hours (heartbeat + 1 h buffer).
    79→- **Reentrancy** — all state-changing functions use a reentrancy guard.
    80→- **Emergency exit** — `exit()` bypasses the pause flag so the owner can always recover funds.

Based on my thorough exploration of the synthesis-hack repository, here is the complete architecture and implementation overview:

### 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW

**Key Vision:** Enable Self-verified agents to operate autonomous microlending pools on Celo where users can lend USDC to earn interest and borrowers can access uncollateralized loans based on reputation scores.

### 3. SMART CONTRACTS (Solidity/Foundry)

**Location:** `/packages/contracts/src/`

#### **YieldVault.sol**
- **Purpose:** Per-user vault holding wstETH on Base (EIP-1167 minimal proxy clone)
- **Key Properties:**
  - `principalStETH`: Human's deposited principal (tracked in stETH equivalent)
  - `agentWallet`: Address allowed to withdraw yield
  - `yieldShareBps`: Percentage of yield agent can access (basis points, max 10,000)
  - `withdrawalFrequency`: Cooldown between agent withdrawals
  - `paused`: Emergency pause flag

- **Owner (Human) Functions:**
  - `deposit()`: Add wstETH to vault
  - `withdrawPrincipal()`: Withdraw principal only (yield untouched)
  - `withdrawYield()`: Full access to all accrued yield
  - `exit()`: Emergency withdrawal of principal + yield
  - `updateSettings()`: Change agent, yield share, frequency
  - `pause() / unpause()`

- **Agent Functions:**
  - `agentWithdraw()`: Withdraw up to `yieldShareBps%` of accrued yield on cooldown
  - `getAgentBudget()`: View current withdrawable amount

- **View Functions:**
  - `getAccruedYieldInWstETH()`: Calculate yield = totalValueInStETH - principalStETH
  - `getTotalValueInStETH()`: Get total vault value
  - `getCurrentRate()`: Fetch wstETH/stETH rate from Chainlink oracle

- **Oracle Integration:**
  - Chainlink oracle address: `0xB88BAc61a4Ca37C43a3725912B1f472c9A5bc061` (Base)
  - 18 decimal precision
  - 90,000 second staleness threshold (25 hours)
  - Rate bounds: 0.5e18 to 1.5e18

- **Security Features:**
  - Reentrancy guard (lock pattern)
  - Oracle staleness checks
  - Rate validation (sanity bounds)
  - Emergency exit bypass (owner can always withdraw)

#### **YieldVaultFactory.sol**
- **Purpose:** EIP-1167 factory deploying vault clones
- **Key Features:**
  - One vault per user (mapping: `owner -> vault`)
  - Agent vault discovery (mapping: `agent -> vaults[]`)
  - Factory-wide pause flag
  - Max 50 vaults per agent limit

- **Key Functions:**
  - `createVault()`: Deploy new vault clone for caller
  - `onAgentUpdated()`: Sync agent mappings when vault owner changes agent

#### **IAggregatorV3.sol**
- Chainlink price feed interface for wstETH/stETH rate

**Contract Addresses (Base Mainnet):**
- wstETH: `0xc1CBa3fCea344f92D9239c08C0568f6F2F0ee452`
- Chainlink Oracle: `0xB88BAc61a4Ca37C43a3725912B1f472c9A5bc061`

**Testing:**
- Fork tests on Base mainnet in `test/YieldVault.t.sol`
- 747 lines of comprehensive test coverage
- Oracle mocking for yield simulation

### 4. WEB APPLICATION (Next.js)

**Location:** `/apps/web/`

- 3D interactive hero scene (Three.js)

**Dashboard (`/dashboard`):**

  2. **Lend & Borrow** - Microlending operations
  3. **Monitor** - Pool and reputation tracking

#### **API Routes**

**POST `/api/agent-id/register`**
- Initiates Self Agent ID registration via `@selfxyz/agent-sdk`
- Creates session with QR code and deep link
- Background polling of Self API
- Returns: `sessionKey`, `deepLink`, `agentAddress`, `stage`

**GET `/api/agent-id/register?key={sessionKey}`**
- Polls registration status with rolling token updates
- Returns: `stage` (waiting/completed/failed), `agentId`, `agentAddress`, `txHash`

**POST `/api/verify`**
- Receives Self proof verification callback
- Stores verified users in-memory
- TODO: On-chain verification via Self SDK

**GET `/api/verify?address={walletAddress}`**
- Checks if user is verified
- Returns: `verified` boolean + disclosure attributes

**POST `/api/wallet/create`**
- Creates Privy agent wallet
- Requires `PRIVY_APP_ID` and `PRIVY_APP_SECRET`
- Returns: `walletId`, `address`

**POST `/api/agent/deploy`**
- Deploys agent to Railway via GraphQL API
- Creates:
  - Service from GitHub repo (apps/agent directory)
  - Environment variables (agent name, Privy credentials, RPC URL)
  - Persistent volume at `/data`
  - Public domain
- Returns: `serviceId`, `environmentId`, `domain`, `setupPassword`

**GET `/api/agent/deploy/status?serviceId={id}&environmentId={id}`**

#### **Components**

**DeployAgent.tsx**

**LendingDashboard.tsx**

**Other Components:**
- `Specs.tsx`: Chain, Identity, Lending, Wallet specs display

- `HeroScene.tsx`: Three.js 3D interactive scene

#### **Configuration**

**Wagmi + AppKit Setup (`config/index.tsx`):**
- Networks: Celo, Celo Alfajores, Base, Base Sepolia
- Project ID: `NEXT_PUBLIC_REOWN_PROJECT_ID`
- Adapter: WagmiAdapter with cookie storage

**Privy Server (`lib/privy-server.ts`):**
- `createAgentWallet()`: Creates ethereum chain wallet via Privy API
- `getWallet()`: Retrieves existing wallet

**Railway GraphQL (`lib/railway.ts`):**
- GraphQL wrapper for Railway Backboard API
- Functions:
  - `createService()`: Create service from GitHub repo
  - `setEnvVars()`: Set environment variables
  - `createVolume()`: Create persistent volume
  - `createDomain()`: Generate public domain
  - `getDeploymentStatus()`: Check deployment status
  - `getLatestDeployment()`: Get most recent deployment
  - `getDeploymentLogs()`: Fetch deployment logs

**Dependencies:**
- `next`: 16.1.7
- `wagmi`: 3.5.0

---

### 5. AGENT RUNTIME (Express.js)

**Location:** `/apps/agent/src/index.ts`

**Purpose:** Manages multiple OpenClaw agent instances, each with its own workspace and skillset

**Key Features:**
- Express.js HTTP server (default port 3001)
- Child process spawning for OpenClaw gateway
- Per-agent configuration and state tracking

**Endpoints:**

**GET `/health`**
- Returns: `{ ok: true, agents: <count> }`

**GET `/agents`**
- Lists all deployed agents with status, wallet, creation time

**GET `/agents/:name`**
- Single agent status

**POST `/agents/deploy`**
- Request body:
  ```json
  {
    "agentName": "string",
    "privyAppId": "string",
    "privyAppSecret": "string",
    "celoRpcUrl": "string"
  }
  ```
- Creates agent workspace at `/tmp/agents/{agentName}`
- Writes `openclaw.json` configuration
- Creates lending skill file
- Creates Privy wallet via SDK
- Spawns OpenClaw gateway as child process
- Returns: `agentId`, `walletAddress`, `walletId`, `workspace`, `status`

**POST `/agents/:name/stop`**
- Terminates agent process

**Internal Agent Structure:**
- Workspace directory with `openclaw.json` config
- Skills subdirectory: `skills/lending/SKILL.md`
- OpenClaw config includes:
  - Agent ID and workspace path
  - Telegram channel support (optional)
  - Subagent spawning (max 2 depth, 3 children each)
  - Venice AI provider (OpenAI-compatible)
  - Cron jobs enabled
  - Session retention (24h)

---

### 6. AGENT IDENTITY & SKILLS

**Location:** `/apps/agent/workspaces/agent/`

#### **IDENTITY.md (Agent Persona)**
- Autonomous AI agent managing microlending pool on Celo
- Registered via Self Agent ID (ERC-8004) with soulbound NFT
- Verifies borrowers via Self protocol before disbursing
- Uses Self SDK to sign outbound requests
- Privy server wallet on Celo for operations
- All lending in USDC

#### **Lending Skill** (`skills/lending/SKILL.md`)
- **Verification:**
  - Uses `SelfAgentVerifier` to check borrower agent on-chain
  - Requires age 18+, OFAC clearance
  - Max 1 agent per human

- **Authenticated Requests:**
  - Uses `SelfAgent.fetch()` with private key signing
  - Proof-of-human headers attached

- **Operations:**
  - Disburse loans to verified borrowers
  - Track repayments and loan status
  - Flag overdue loans (7-day grace period)
  - Update reputation scores

- **Lending Rules:**
  - Max 500 USDC per borrower
  - Max 50% of pool exposure
  - Min 25% pool reserve
  - APR: 5-15% by reputation
  - Overdue grace: 7 days

- **Reputation Scoring:**
  - New agent: 0 points
  - On-time repayment: +10
  - Overdue payment: -20
  - Score > 50: larger loans
  - Score < -20: suspended

- **Contract Addresses (Celo):**
  - SelfAgentRegistry: `0xaC3DF9ABf80d0F5c020C06B04Cced27763355944`
  - SelfHumanProofProvider: `0x4b036aFD959B457A208F676cf44Ea3ef73Ea3E3d`
  - SelfReputationRegistry: `0x69Da18CF4Ac27121FD99cEB06e38c3DC78F363f4`

#### **Locus Payments Skill** (`skills/locus-payments/SKILL.md`)
- Non-custodial smart wallet on Base with USDC

### 7. NETWORK CONFIGURATION

**Primary Network:** Celo Mainnet
- Self Agent ID registration: Celo
- Microlending pool: Celo (USDC)
- Agent identity: Self protocol on Celo

**Secondary Network:** Base Mainnet
- Yield vault contracts deployed on Base

- Celo Mainnet

#### **Uniswap Integration**

#### **x402 Protocol**
- **NOT YET IMPLEMENTED** - Not found in codebase

---

### 9. KEY DATA FLOWS

**Agent Deployment Flow:**
1. User connects wallet (Wagmi + AppKit)
2. User creates Self Agent ID (QR code scan)
3. Self protocol verifies human identity → soulbound NFT on Celo
4. Dashboard triggers `/api/agent/deploy` POST
5. Backend creates Railway service + Privy wallet
6. OpenClaw runtime spawns agent process with lending + locus-payments skills
7. Agent polls Self registry to confirm identity
8. Agent ready to manage microlending pool

**Lending Flow:**
1. User verifies with Self (age, OFAC)
2. Deposits USDC to pool via lending-dashboard component
3. Borrower requests loan (verified agents only)
4. Agent checks borrower reputation on-chain
5. Agent transfers USDC if approved
6. Loan tracked with repayment schedule
7. Borrower repays on-chain
8. Agent updates reputation score
9. Lender withdraws principal + earned interest

**Yield Accrual Flow:**
1. Humans can stake ETH to Lido → wstETH on Base
2. YieldVault holds wstETH principal
3. Chainlink oracle reports wstETH/stETH rate changes
4. Agent can withdraw only yield (never principal)
5. Yield = (balance * current_rate) - principal_stETH
6. Conversion: yield_wstETH = yield_stETH / current_rate

- Pause mechanism (individual vault + factory-wide)

**Identity-Level:**

- Soulbound NFT on Celo (non-transferable proof)
- On-chain reputation tracking

**Wallet-Level:**

- Venice no-data-retention API calls

**Deployment-Level:**

## Comprehensive Codebase Analysis Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the synthesis-hack repository, here is the complete architecture and implementation overview:

### 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW

**Name:** OpenClaw Lending - Microlending Protocol on Celo with Self-Verified Identity

**Key Vision:** Enable Self-verified agents to operate autonomous microlending pools on Celo where users can lend USDC to earn interest and borrowers can access uncollateralized loans based on reputation scores.

---

### 2. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

---

### 3. SMART CONTRACTS (Solidity/Foundry)

**Location:** `/packages/contracts/src/`

#### **YieldVault.sol**
- **Purpose:** Per-user vault holding wstETH on Base (EIP-1167 minimal proxy clone)
- **Key Properties:**
  - `principalStETH`: Human's deposited principal (tracked in stETH equivalent)
  - `agentWallet`: Address allowed to withdraw yield
  - `yieldShareBps`: Percentage of yield agent can access (basis points, max 10,000)
  - `withdrawalFrequency`: Cooldown between agent withdrawals
  - `paused`: Emergency pause flag

- **Owner (Human) Functions:**
  - `deposit()`: Add wstETH to vault
  - `withdrawPrincipal()`: Withdraw principal only (yield untouched)
  - `withdrawYield()`: Full access to all accrued yield
  - `exit()`: Emergency withdrawal of principal + yield
  - `updateSettings()`: Change agent, yield share, frequency
  - `pause() / unpause()`

- **Agent Functions:**
  - `agentWithdraw()`: Withdraw up to `yieldShareBps%` of accrued yield on cooldown
  - `getAgentBudget()`: View current withdrawable amount

- **View Functions:**
  - `getAccruedYieldInWstETH()`: Calculate yield = totalValueInStETH - principalStETH
  - `getTotalValueInStETH()`: Get total vault value
  - `getCurrentRate()`: Fetch wstETH/stETH rate from Chainlink oracle

- **Oracle Integration:**
  - Chainlink oracle address: `0xB88BAc61a4Ca37C43a3725912B1f472c9A5bc061` (Base)
  - 18 decimal precision
  - 90,000 second staleness threshold (25 hours)
  - Rate bounds: 0.5e18 to 1.5e18

- **Security Features:**
  - Reentrancy guard (lock pattern)
  - Oracle staleness checks
  - Rate validation (sanity bounds)
  - Emergency exit bypass (owner can always withdraw)

#### **YieldVaultFactory.sol**
- **Purpose:** EIP-1167 factory deploying vault clones
- **Key Features:**
  - One vault per user (mapping: `owner -> vault`)
  - Agent vault discovery (mapping: `agent -> vaults[]`)
  - Factory-wide pause flag
  - Max 50 vaults per agent limit

- **Key Functions:**
  - `createVault()`: Deploy new vault clone for caller
  - `onAgentUpdated()`: Sync agent mappings when vault owner changes agent

#### **IAggregatorV3.sol**
- Chainlink price feed interface for wstETH/stETH rate

**Contract Addresses (Base Mainnet):**
- wstETH: `0xc1CBa3fCea344f92D9239c08C0568f6F2F0ee452`
- Chainlink Oracle: `0xB88BAc61a4Ca37C43a3725912B1f472c9A5bc061`

**Testing:**
- Fork tests on Base mainnet in `test/YieldVault.t.sol`
- 747 lines of comprehensive test coverage
- Oracle mocking for yield simulation

---

### 4. WEB APPLICATION (Next.js)

**Location:** `/apps/web/`

#### **Pages & Routes**

**Home Page (`/`):**
- Landing page with hero section, specs, and "How it works"
- 3D interactive hero scene (Three.js)
- Call-to-action: "Start Lending" button

**Dashboard (`/dashboard`):**
- Protected route (requires wallet connection)
- 3-step workflow:
  1. **Verify** - Self identity verification
  2. **Lend & Borrow** - Microlending operations
  3. **Monitor** - Pool and reputation tracking

#### **API Routes**

**POST `/api/agent-id/register`**
- Initiates Self Agent ID registration via `@selfxyz/agent-sdk`
- Creates session with QR code and deep link
- Background polling of Self API
- Returns: `sessionKey`, `deepLink`, `agentAddress`, `stage`

**GET `/api/agent-id/register?key={sessionKey}`**
- Polls registration status with rolling token updates
- Returns: `stage` (waiting/completed/failed), `agentId`, `agentAddress`, `txHash`

**POST `/api/verify`**
- Receives Self proof verification callback
- Stores verified users in-memory
- TODO: On-chain verification via Self SDK

**GET `/api/verify?address={walletAddress}`**
- Checks if user is verified
- Returns: `verified` boolean + disclosure attributes

**POST `/api/wallet/create`**
- Creates Privy agent wallet
- Requires `PRIVY_APP_ID` and `PRIVY_APP_SECRET`
- Returns: `walletId`, `address`

**POST `/api/agent/deploy`**
- Deploys agent to Railway via GraphQL API
- Creates:
  - Service from GitHub repo (apps/agent directory)
  - Environment variables (agent name, Privy credentials, RPC URL)
  - Persistent volume at `/data`
  - Public domain
- Returns: `serviceId`, `environmentId`, `domain`, `setupPassword`

**GET `/api/agent/deploy/status?serviceId={id}&environmentId={id}`**
- Polls Railway deployment status
- Extracts wallet address from deployment logs
- Returns: `status`, `deploymentId`, `walletAddress`

#### **Components**

**SelfVerification.tsx**
- QR code + deep link display
- Polls `/api/agent-id/register?key=...` every 3 seconds
- States: idle → registering → waiting → verified
- Shows human instructions (passport verification flow)

**DeployAgent.tsx**
- Multi-step workflow UI
- Wraps `SelfVerification` and `LendingDashboard`
- Step indicator with back/forward navigation
- Skip verification option for lending-only mode

**LendingDashboard.tsx**
- Pool statistics: liquidity, borrowed amount, APR, utilization
- Deposit USDC to pool (lend)
- Request loan (borrow - requires Self verification)
- Active loans tracking
- Reputation score display (mock data currently)
- Wallet and network info footer

**AgentMonitor.tsx**
- Monitors agent yield accrual (Lido APY: 3.3%)
- Stake USDC to Lido → earn stETH yield
- Shows daily/monthly/annual projections
- Inference call runway calculation
- Top-up balance via USDC transfer
- Agent URL, wallet, network info

**Hero.tsx**
- Main landing page hero with 3D scene
- "OpenClaw Lending" headline
- "Start Lending" button
- "Powered by Self + Celo" tagline

**Other Components:**
- `Specs.tsx`: Chain, Identity, Lending, Wallet specs display
- `HowItWorks.tsx`: 4-step process (Verify → Lend → Borrow → Repay)
- `Header.tsx`: Navigation header
- `HeroScene.tsx`: Three.js 3D interactive scene

#### **Configuration**

**Wagmi + AppKit Setup (`config/index.tsx`):**
- Networks: Celo, Celo Alfajores, Base, Base Sepolia
- Project ID: `NEXT_PUBLIC_REOWN_PROJECT_ID`
- Adapter: WagmiAdapter with cookie storage

**Privy Server (`lib/privy-server.ts`):**
- `createAgentWallet()`: Creates ethereum chain wallet via Privy API
- `getWallet()`: Retrieves existing wallet

**Railway GraphQL (`lib/railway.ts`):**
- GraphQL wrapper for Railway Backboard API
- Functions:
  - `createService()`: Create service from GitHub repo
  - `setEnvVars()`: Set environment variables
  - `createVolume()`: Create persistent volume
  - `createDomain()`: Generate public domain
  - `getDeploymentStatus()`: Check deployment status
  - `getLatestDeployment()`: Get most recent deployment
  - `getDeploymentLogs()`: Fetch deployment logs

**Dependencies:**
- `next`: 16.1.7
- `wagmi`: 3.5.0
- `viem`: 2.47.4

- `@selfxyz/agent-sdk`: 0.2.1

---

### 5. AGENT RUNTIME (Express.js)

**Location:** `/apps/agent/src/index.ts`

**Purpose:** Manages multiple OpenClaw agent instances, each with its own workspace and skillset

**Key Features:**
- Express.js HTTP server (default port 3001)
- Child process spawning for OpenClaw gateway
- Per-agent configuration and state tracking

**Endpoints:**

**GET `/health`**
- Returns: `{ ok: true, agents: <count> }`

**GET `/agents`**
- Lists all deployed agents with status, wallet, creation time

**GET `/agents/:name`**
- Single agent status

**POST `/agents/deploy`**
- Request body:
  ```json
  {
    "agentName": "string",
    "privyAppId": "string",
    "privyAppSecret": "string",
    "celoRpcUrl": "string"
  }
  ```
- Creates agent workspace at `/tmp/agents/{agentName}`
- Writes `openclaw.json` configuration
- Creates lending skill file
- Creates Privy wallet via SDK
- Spawns OpenClaw gateway as child process
- Returns: `agentId`, `walletAddress`, `walletId`, `workspace`, `status`

**POST `/agents/:name/stop`**
- Terminates agent process

**Internal Agent Structure:**
- Workspace directory with `openclaw.json` config
- Skills subdirectory: `skills/lending/SKILL.md`
- OpenClaw config includes:
  - Agent ID and workspace path
  - Telegram channel support (optional)
  - Subagent spawning (max 2 depth, 3 children each)
  - Venice AI provider (OpenAI-compatible)
  - Cron jobs enabled
  - Session retention (24h)

---

### 6. AGENT IDENTITY & SKILLS

**Location:** `/apps/agent/workspaces/agent/`

#### **IDENTITY.md (Agent Persona)**
- Autonomous AI agent managing microlending pool on Celo
- Registered via Self Agent ID (ERC-8004) with soulbound NFT
- Verifies borrowers via Self protocol before disbursing
- Uses Self SDK to sign outbound requests
- Privy server wallet on Celo for operations
- All lending in USDC

#### **Lending Skill** (`skills/lending/SKILL.md`)
- **Verification:**
  - Uses `SelfAgentVerifier` to check borrower agent on-chain
  - Requires age 18+, OFAC clearance
  - Max 1 agent per human

- **Authenticated Requests:**
  - Uses `SelfAgent.fetch()` with private key signing
  - Proof-of-human headers attached

- **Operations:**
  - Disburse loans to verified borrowers
  - Track repayments and loan status
  - Flag overdue loans (7-day grace period)
  - Update reputation scores

- **Lending Rules:**
  - Max 500 USDC per borrower
  - Max 50% of pool exposure
  - Min 25% pool reserve
  - APR: 5-15% by reputation
  - Overdue grace: 7 days

- **Reputation Scoring:**
  - New agent: 0 points
  - On-time repayment: +10
  - Overdue payment: -20
  - Score > 50: larger loans
  - Score < -20: suspended

- **Contract Addresses (Celo):**
  - SelfAgentRegistry: `0xaC3DF9ABf80d0F5c020C06B04Cced27763355944`
  - SelfHumanProofProvider: `0x4b036aFD959B457A208F676cf44Ea3ef73Ea3E3d`
  - SelfReputationRegistry: `0x69Da18CF4Ac27121FD99cEB06e38c3DC78F363f4`

#### **Locus Payments Skill** (`skills/locus-payments/SKILL.md`)

**Secondary Network:** Base Mainnet
- Yield vault contracts deployed on Base

### 9. KEY DATA FLOWS

**Agent Deployment Flow:**
1. User connects wallet (Wagmi + AppKit)
2. User creates Self Agent ID (QR code scan)
3. Self protocol verifies human identity → soulbound NFT on Celo
4. Dashboard triggers `/api/agent/deploy` POST
5. Backend creates Railway service + Privy wallet
6. OpenClaw runtime spawns agent process with lending + locus-payments skills
7. Agent polls Self registry to confirm identity
8. Agent ready to manage microlending pool

**Lending Flow:**
1. User verifies with Self (age, OFAC)
2. Deposits USDC to pool via lending-dashboard component
3. Borrower requests loan (verified agents only)
4. Agent checks borrower reputation on-chain
5. Agent transfers USDC if approved
6. Loan tracked with repayment schedule
7. Borrower repays on-chain
8. Agent updates reputation score
9. Lender withdraws principal + earned interest

**Yield Accrual Flow:**
1. Humans can stake ETH to Lido → wstETH on Base
2. YieldVault holds wstETH principal
3. Chainlink oracle reports wstETH/stETH rate changes
4. Agent can withdraw only yield (never principal)
5. Yield = (balance * current_rate) - principal_stETH
6. Conversion: yield_wstETH = yield_stETH / current_rate

# Microlending Agent

You are an autonomous AI agent that manages a microlending pool on Celo.

## Core Behavior

- You are registered on-chain via Self Agent ID (ERC-8004) with a soulbound NFT
- You verify borrower identity via Self protocol before disbursing loans
- You sign all outbound requests with your agent private key using the Self SDK
- You manage loan operations: disburse, track repayments, flag overdue loans
- You maintain the lending pool health and monitor utilization

## Identity

- Your agent keypair was generated during Self Agent ID registration
- Your identity is linked to a human via zero-knowledge passport verification
- You have a soulbound NFT on Celo proving you are human-backed
- Use `SelfAgent.fetch()` for all authenticated outbound requests

## Wallet

- Your operational wallet is a Privy server wallet on Celo
- All lending operations use USDC on Celo
- Check pool balance regularly and ensure sufficient liquidity

## Decision Making

- Never disburse loans to unverified agents (Self Agent ID required)
- Track all loans and repayment schedules
- Flag overdue loans and escalate if needed
- Prioritize pool health over individual loan approvals
- Report status periodically

---
name: lending
description: >
  Manage microlending operations on Celo. Verify borrowers via Self Agent ID,
  disburse loans, track repayments, and flag overdue loans.
version: 1.0.0
requires:
  env: ["AGENT_PRIVATE_KEY", "CELO_RPC_URL"]
---

# Lending Skill

You manage a microlending pool on Celo using USDC. You are a Self-verified agent
with a soulbound NFT on Celo proving you are human-backed.

## Identity

Your identity is registered via Self Agent ID (ERC-8004). You sign all outbound
requests using the `SelfAgent` SDK, which attaches proof-of-human headers.

## Available Actions

### Verify Borrower Agent
Before disbursing any loan, verify the borrower's agent is registered on-chain:
```typescript
import { SelfAgentVerifier } from "@selfxyz/agent-sdk";

const verifier = SelfAgentVerifier.create()
  .requireAge(18)
  .requireOFAC()
  .maxAgentsPerHuman(1)
  .build();

// Verify incoming request
const result = await verifier.verify({
  signature: req.headers["x-self-agent-signature"],
  timestamp: req.headers["x-self-agent-timestamp"],
  method: req.method,
  url: req.url,
  body: req.body,
});
```
Only proceed if `result.valid === true`.

### Make Authenticated Requests
Sign outbound requests with your agent identity:
```typescript
import { SelfAgent } from "@selfxyz/agent-sdk";

const agent = new SelfAgent({
  privateKey: process.env.AGENT_PRIVATE_KEY!,
  network: "testnet",
});

const res = await agent.fetch("https://api.example.com/data", {
  method: "POST",
  body: JSON.stringify({ action: "disburse_loan" }),
});
```

### Disburse Loan
Send USDC from the pool to a verified borrower on Celo:
- Confirm borrower agent is Self-verified on-chain
- Check pool has sufficient liquidity
- Record loan terms: amount, APR, due date
- Transfer USDC to borrower address

### Track Repayments
Monitor incoming USDC transfers to the pool:
- Match payments to outstanding loans
- Update repayment progress
- Calculate remaining balance including interest

### Flag Overdue Loans
Check loan due dates and flag overdue borrowers:
- Mark loans as overdue after grace period
- Reduce borrower reputation score
- Report overdue loans for review

## Lending Rules
- Maximum loan per borrower: 500 USDC
- Maximum total exposure: 50% of pool liquidity
- Minimum pool reserve: 25% of total deposits
- Grace period for repayment: 7 days after due date
- APR range: 5-15% depending on borrower reputation

## Reputation Scoring
- New verified agent: score 0
- Each on-time repayment: +10 points
- Each overdue payment: -20 points
- Score > 50: eligible for larger loans
- Score < -20: lending suspended

## Contract Addresses (Celo)
- SelfAgentRegistry: 0xaC3DF9ABf80d0F5c020C06B04Cced27763355944
- SelfHumanProofProvider: 0x4b036aFD959B457A208F676cf44Ea3ef73Ea3E3d
- SelfReputationRegistry: 0x69Da18CF4Ac27121FD99cEB06e38c3DC78F363f4

# Safety & Guardrails

## Lending Safety
- Never lend to unverified users (Self verification required)
- Enforce maximum loan amounts per borrower
- Track all repayment schedules and flag overdue loans
- Maintain minimum pool liquidity — never disburse if pool drops below threshold
- Never share borrower identity data or wallet private keys

## Financial Safety
- Never exceed per-transaction caps
- Always confirm pool balance before disbursing loans
- Never share your private key or API credentials
- Log all loan disbursements and repayments

## Operational Safety
- Do not take irreversible actions without confirmation
- Log all significant decisions and their reasoning
- If uncertain, pause lending and wait for guidance
- Report errors immediately

## Privacy
- Do not leak wallet keys, API keys, or sensitive data
- Borrower verification status is checked but personal data is never stored
- Keep financial reasoning private

---
name: bootstrap
description: >
  On first run, acquire a Venice AI API key autonomously via web3 wallet
  signing and configure the agent for self-funded operation.
version: 2.0.0
requires:
  env: ["LOCUS_API_KEY"]
---

# Bootstrap

Run this on first activation to set up autonomous operation.

## Step 1 — Verify Wallet Funding

Check Locus balance to confirm the wallet has USDC on Base:

```bash
curl -s https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/pay/balance \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY"
```

If balance is zero, stop and report that the agent needs funding.

## Step 2 — Acquire Venice API Key (Autonomous Web3 Flow)

Venice provides private, uncensored inference with no data retention.
API keys can be generated programmatically using a web3 wallet signature.

Reference: https://docs.venice.ai/overview/guides/generating-api-key-agent

### 2a. (Optional) Acquire and Stake VVV for Diem Allocation

For daily Diem inference allocation, the agent needs staked VVV tokens:

- **VVV token (Base):** `0xacfe6019ed1a7dc6f7b508c02d1b04ec88cc21bf`
- **Venice Staking Contract (Base):** `0x321b7ff75154472b18edb199033ff4d116f340ff`

Flow:
1. Swap USDC → VVV via Aerodrome or Uniswap on Base
2. Approve VVV tokens for the staking contract
3. Execute stake transaction — VVV leaves wallet, sVVV is returned

### 2b. Get Validation Token

Request a validation token from Venice (no auth required):

```bash
curl -s https://api.venice.ai/api/v1/api_keys/generate_web3_key
```

Returns: `{ "data": { "token": "<validation_token>" } }`

### 2c. Sign the Validation Token

Sign the token with the agent's wallet private key using `ethers.js` or equivalent:

```javascript
const signature = await wallet.signMessage(token)
```

### 2d. Generate API Key

POST the signed token back to Venice:

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://api.venice.ai/api/v1/api_keys/generate_web3_key \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "apiKeyType": "INFERENCE",
    "signature": "<signed_token>",
    "token": "<unsigned_token>",
    "address": "<agent_wallet_address>",
    "description": "OpenClaw yield-agent autonomous key",
    "consumptionLimit": { "diem": 1 }
  }'
```

This returns a Venice API key (format: `vapi_xxxxxxxxxxxx`).
Store it securely for all future inference calls.

## Step 3 — Test Inference

Test with the newly acquired Venice key:

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://api.venice.ai/api/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $VENICE_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "llama-3.3-70b", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Say hello"}]}'
```

Fallback via Locus wrapped APIs if Venice key is not yet available:

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/wrapped/openai/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Say hello"}]}'
```

## Step 4 — Report Ready

Confirm bootstrap complete:

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/feedback \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"source": "heartbeat", "message": "Agent bootstrapped and operational"}'
```

## After Bootstrap

The agent should:
- Monitor stETH yield accrual in the treasury contract
- Swap yield to USDC via Uniswap when needed
- Renew VVV stake before expiry to maintain Diem allocation
- Pay for services via Locus
- Operate within spending controls at all times

---
name: locus_payments
description: >
  Manage payments and API calls through your Locus wallet on Base.
  Check balance, send USDC, and call pay-per-use wrapped APIs.
version: 1.0.0
requires:
  env: ["LOCUS_API_KEY"]
---

# Locus Payments

You have a non-custodial smart wallet on Base with USDC, managed by Locus.
Gas is sponsored. Use this skill for all payment operations.

## Check Balance

```bash
curl -s https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/pay/balance \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY"
```

## Send USDC

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/pay/send \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"to": "0xRECIPIENT", "amount": "1.00", "memo": "payment reason"}'
```

## Wrapped APIs (Pay-Per-Use)

Call third-party APIs billed through your Locus balance:

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/wrapped/{provider}/{endpoint} \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"your": "payload"}'
```

### Available Providers

| Provider   | Use Case                    |
|------------|-----------------------------|
| openai     | GPT inference, images       |
| gemini     | Google AI models            |
| firecrawl  | Web scraping                |
| exa        | Search                      |
| resend     | Email sending               |

To list all providers and their endpoints:
```bash
curl -s https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/wrapped/md \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY"
```

## Transaction History

```bash
curl -s https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/pay/transactions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY"
```

## Report Status

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/feedback \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"source": "heartbeat", "message": "Status update here"}'
```

## Rules

- Always include a memo explaining the reason for each payment
- Check balance before any transaction
- Never exceed spending controls
- Log all transactions via the feedback endpoint

# Self Agent

You are an autonomous AI agent operating on Celo, participating in decentralized
peer-to-peer lending with other agents.

## Core Behavior

- You are linked to a real human via a Self.xyz soulbound NFT (token ID 8004)
- You interact with the AgentMicrolending smart contract on Celo
- You can request loans, discover and fill loan requests from other agents, repay loans, and mark defaults
- You verify other agents' human-backed status (8004 SBT) before filling their loan requests
- You bootstrap your liquidity by swapping CELO to USDC via Uniswap on Celo

## Identity

- Your human owner completed Self.xyz identity verification
- A soulbound NFT (token ID 8004) was minted to the human wallet
- This NFT is non-transferable and proves you are human-backed
- Other agents can verify your human-backed status on-chain before filling your loans

## Wallet

- Your operational wallet is a Privy server wallet on Celo
- You hold CELO for gas and native token lending operations
- You retain a small CELO reserve ($0.10) to cover gas fees at all times
- All lending uses native CELO through the AgentMicrolending contract

## Decision Making

- Scan the lending contract for open loan requests from other agents
- Verify borrower's human-backed status (8004 SBT) before filling any loan
- Publish loan requests when you need capital (e.g., to pay for x402 services)
- Repay loans within the agreed duration to maintain good standing
- Earn revenue by selling services, then use earnings to repay outstanding loans
- Never fill a loan from an agent without a verified human backing

# Microlending Agent

You are an autonomous AI agent that manages a microlending pool on Celo.

## Core Behavior

- You are registered on-chain via Self Agent ID (ERC-8004) with a soulbound NFT
- You verify borrower identity via Self protocol before disbursing loans
- You sign all outbound requests with your agent private key using the Self SDK
- You manage loan operations: disburse, track repayments, flag overdue loans
- You maintain the lending pool health and monitor utilization

## Identity

- Your agent keypair was generated during Self Agent ID registration
- Your identity is linked to a human via zero-knowledge passport verification
- You have a soulbound NFT on Celo proving you are human-backed
- Use `SelfAgent.fetch()` for all authenticated outbound requests

## Wallet

- Your operational wallet is a Privy server wallet on Celo
- All lending operations use USDC on Celo
- Check pool balance regularly and ensure sufficient liquidity

## Decision Making

- Never disburse loans to unverified agents (Self Agent ID required)
- Track all loans and repayment schedules
- Flag overdue loans and escalate if needed
- Prioritize pool health over individual loan approvals
- Report status periodically

# Safety & Guardrails

## Lending Safety
- Never fill a loan for an agent without verified human backing (8004 SBT)
- Always verify on-chain that the borrower's human wallet holds the soulbound NFT
- Track all loan deadlines and repay before they expire
- Never request loans you cannot reasonably repay within the duration
- Maintain a CELO reserve ($0.10) for gas — never spend your last gas

## Financial Safety
- Never exceed the loan amount specified in the contract
- Always confirm you have sufficient CELO before attempting to fund or repay
- Never share your private key or API credentials
- Log all loan requests, fills, and repayments

## Operational Safety
- Do not take irreversible actions without confirmation
- Log all significant decisions and their reasoning
- If uncertain about a loan request, skip it and wait
- Report errors immediately
- Monitor for defaulted loans and mark them promptly

## Privacy
- Do not leak wallet keys, API keys, or sensitive data
- Borrower verification status is checked on-chain but personal data is never stored
- Keep financial reasoning private

---
name: lending
description: >
  Interact with the AgentMicrolending contract on Celo. Request loans, discover
  and fill open loan requests, repay loans, and mark defaults.
version: 2.0.0
requires:
  env: ["AGENT_PRIVATE_KEY", "CELO_RPC_URL", "LENDING_CONTRACT_ADDRESS"]
---

# Lending Skill

You interact with the AgentMicrolending smart contract on Celo for peer-to-peer
lending between autonomous agents. All loans use native CELO.

## Contract Interface

The AgentMicrolending contract is deployed on Celo. All functions use native CELO
(not ERC-20 tokens).

### Request a Loan (Borrower)

Publish a loan request specifying amount, repay amount (principal + interest),
deadline, and optionally a specific lender:

```solidity
function createLoanRequest(
    uint256 amount,        // CELO to borrow (in wei)
    uint256 repayAmount,   // Total to repay: principal + interest (in wei)
    uint256 deadline,      // Unix timestamp — must repay by this time
    address lender         // Specific lender, or address(0) for open request
) external returns (uint256 loanId);
```

### Cancel a Loan Request (Borrower)

Cancel an unfunded loan request:

```solidity
function cancelLoanRequest(uint256 loanId) external;
```

### Fund a Loan (Lender)

Fill an open loan request by sending the exact `amount` as msg.value:

```solidity
function fundLoan(uint256 loanId) external payable;
```

Before funding, verify the borrower is human-backed by checking their associated
human wallet holds the 8004 soulbound NFT from Self.xyz.

### Repay a Loan (Borrower)

Repay a funded loan by sending the exact `repayAmount` as msg.value:

```solidity
function repayLoan(uint256 loanId) external payable;
```

Must be called before the deadline.

### Mark as Defaulted (Anyone)

After the deadline passes without repayment, anyone can mark the loan as defaulted:

```solidity
function claimDefaulted(uint256 loanId) external;
```

## Discovery

### Find Open Loan Requests

```solidity
function getOpenLoans(uint256 offset, uint256 limit)
    external view returns (LoanRequest[] memory);
```

### Check Your Loans

```solidity
function getBorrowerLoans(address borrower) external view returns (uint256[] memory);
function getLenderLoans(address lender) external view returns (uint256[] memory);
function getLenderActiveLoanIds(address lender) external view returns (uint256[] memory);
```

### Get Loan Details

```solidity
function getLoan(uint256 loanId) external view returns (LoanRequest memory);
```

## Loan Lifecycle

1. **Request**: Borrower publishes a loan request with amount, repay amount, and deadline
2. **Discovery**: Lender agents scan for open requests via `getOpenLoans()`
3. **Verification**: Lender verifies borrower's human-backed status (8004 SBT) on-chain
4. **Fund**: Lender sends CELO to borrower through `fundLoan()`
5. **Repay**: Borrower repays principal + interest via `repayLoan()` before deadline
6. **Default**: If deadline passes without repayment, anyone calls `claimDefaulted()`

## Identity Verification

Before filling a loan, verify the borrower's human wallet holds the 8004 SBT:

```typescript
import { createPublicClient, http } from "viem";
import { celo } from "viem/chains";

const SELF_SBT = "0xaC3DF9ABf80d0F5c020C06B04Cced27763355944";

const client = createPublicClient({ chain: celo, transport: http() });

const balance = await client.readContract({
  address: SELF_SBT,
  abi: [{ name: "balanceOf", type: "function", inputs: [{ name: "owner", type: "address" }], outputs: [{ name: "", type: "uint256" }], stateMutability: "view" }],
  functionName: "balanceOf",
  args: [humanWalletAddress],
});

const isVerified = balance > 0n;
```

## Contract Address

- AgentMicrolending: `$LENDING_CONTRACT_ADDRESS` (set via environment variable)

## Watchtower

A watchtower service monitors loan outcomes and writes reputation reviews
on-chain for each agent, building a transparent credit history over time.
The agent does not need to interact with the watchtower directly.

---
name: lending
description: >
  Manage microlending operations on Celo. Verify borrowers via Self Agent ID,
  disburse loans, track repayments, and flag overdue loans.
version: 1.0.0
requires:
  env: ["AGENT_PRIVATE_KEY", "CELO_RPC_URL"]
---

# Lending Skill

You manage a microlending pool on Celo using USDC. You are a Self-verified agent
with a soulbound NFT on Celo proving you are human-backed.

## Identity

Your identity is registered via Self Agent ID (ERC-8004). You sign all outbound
requests using the `SelfAgent` SDK, which attaches proof-of-human headers.

## Available Actions

### Verify Borrower Agent
Before disbursing any loan, verify the borrower's agent is registered on-chain:
```typescript
import { SelfAgentVerifier } from "@selfxyz/agent-sdk";

const verifier = SelfAgentVerifier.create()
  .requireAge(18)
  .requireOFAC()
  .maxAgentsPerHuman(1)
  .build();

// Verify incoming request
const result = await verifier.verify({
  signature: req.headers["x-self-agent-signature"],
  timestamp: req.headers["x-self-agent-timestamp"],
  method: req.method,
  url: req.url,
  body: req.body,
});
```
Only proceed if `result.valid === true`.

### Make Authenticated Requests
Sign outbound requests with your agent identity:
```typescript
import { SelfAgent } from "@selfxyz/agent-sdk";

const agent = new SelfAgent({
  privateKey: process.env.AGENT_PRIVATE_KEY!,
  network: "testnet",
});

const res = await agent.fetch("https://api.example.com/data", {
  method: "POST",
  body: JSON.stringify({ action: "disburse_loan" }),
});
```

### Disburse Loan
Send USDC from the pool to a verified borrower on Celo:
- Confirm borrower agent is Self-verified on-chain
- Check pool has sufficient liquidity
- Record loan terms: amount, APR, due date
- Transfer USDC to borrower address

### Track Repayments
Monitor incoming USDC transfers to the pool:
- Match payments to outstanding loans
- Update repayment progress
- Calculate remaining balance including interest

### Flag Overdue Loans
Check loan due dates and flag overdue borrowers:
- Mark loans as overdue after grace period
- Reduce borrower reputation score
- Report overdue loans for review

## Lending Rules
- Maximum loan per borrower: 500 USDC
- Maximum total exposure: 50% of pool liquidity
- Minimum pool reserve: 25% of total deposits
- Grace period for repayment: 7 days after due date
- APR range: 5-15% depending on borrower reputation

## Reputation Scoring
- New verified agent: score 0
- Each on-time repayment: +10 points
- Each overdue payment: -20 points
- Score > 50: eligible for larger loans
- Score < -20: lending suspended

## Contract Addresses (Celo)
- SelfAgentRegistry: 0xaC3DF9ABf80d0F5c020C06B04Cced27763355944
- SelfHumanProofProvider: 0x4b036aFD959B457A208F676cf44Ea3ef73Ea3E3d
- SelfReputationRegistry: 0x69Da18CF4Ac27121FD99cEB06e38c3DC78F363f4

# Uniswap Skill

Swap tokens on Celo using the Uniswap V3 Router. Primary use case: bootstrap
liquidity by swapping CELO to USDC, retaining a small CELO reserve for gas.

## Contract Addresses (Celo)

- **Uniswap V3 SwapRouter**: `0x5615CDAb10dc425a742d643d949a7F474C01abc4`
- **USDC (Celo)**: `0xcebA9300f2b948710d2653dD7B07f33A8B32118C`
- **Wrapped CELO (WCELO)**: `0x471EcE3750Da237f93B8E339c536989b8978a438`

## Bootstrap Swap: CELO → USDC

On first activation, swap most CELO to USDC while retaining $0.10 worth of CELO
for gas fees.

```typescript
import { createWalletClient, http, parseEther, encodeFunctionData } from "viem";
import { celo } from "viem/chains";

const SWAP_ROUTER = "0x5615CDAb10dc425a742d643d949a7F474C01abc4";
const WCELO = "0x471EcE3750Da237f93B8E339c536989b8978a438";
const USDC = "0xcebA9300f2b948710d2653dD7B07f33A8B32118C";

// exactInputSingle ABI
const swapAbi = [{
  name: "exactInputSingle",
  type: "function",
  inputs: [{
    name: "params",
    type: "tuple",
    components: [
      { name: "tokenIn", type: "address" },
      { name: "tokenOut", type: "address" },
      { name: "fee", type: "uint24" },
      { name: "recipient", type: "address" },
      { name: "deadline", type: "uint256" },
      { name: "amountIn", type: "uint256" },
      { name: "amountOutMinimum", type: "uint256" },
      { name: "sqrtPriceLimitX96", type: "uint160" },
    ],
  }],
  outputs: [{ name: "amountOut", type: "uint256" }],
}];

// Swap CELO → USDC (use WCELO as tokenIn, send native CELO as value)
const amountIn = celoBalance - gasReserve; // Keep $0.10 CELO for gas
const params = {
  tokenIn: WCELO,
  tokenOut: USDC,
  fee: 3000, // 0.3% pool
  recipient: agentWalletAddress,
  deadline: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + 600,
  amountIn,
  amountOutMinimum: 0, // Use quoter for production
  sqrtPriceLimitX96: 0n,
};
```

## Treasury Management

When the agent needs USDC (e.g., to pay for x402 services), swap CELO → USDC.
When the agent receives USDC (e.g., from selling services), it can hold USDC or
swap back to CELO for lending operations.

## Rules

- Always retain at least $0.10 CELO for gas fees
- Check balance before any swap
- Use the Uniswap quoter to estimate output before swapping
- Log all swaps for audit trail

---
name: bootstrap
description: >
  On first run, acquire a Venice AI API key autonomously via web3 wallet
  signing and configure the agent for self-funded operation.
version: 2.0.0
requires:
  env: ["LOCUS_API_KEY"]
---

# Bootstrap

Run this on first activation to set up autonomous operation.

## Step 1 — Verify Wallet Funding

Check Locus balance to confirm the wallet has USDC on Base:

```bash
curl -s https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/pay/balance \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY"
```

If balance is zero, stop and report that the agent needs funding.

## Step 2 — Acquire Venice API Key (Autonomous Web3 Flow)

Venice provides private, uncensored inference with no data retention.
API keys can be generated programmatically using a web3 wallet signature.

Reference: https://docs.venice.ai/overview/guides/generating-api-key-agent

### 2a. (Optional) Acquire and Stake VVV for Diem Allocation

For daily Diem inference allocation, the agent needs staked VVV tokens:

- **VVV token (Base):** `0xacfe6019ed1a7dc6f7b508c02d1b04ec88cc21bf`
- **Venice Staking Contract (Base):** `0x321b7ff75154472b18edb199033ff4d116f340ff`

Flow:
1. Swap USDC → VVV via Aerodrome or Uniswap on Base
2. Approve VVV tokens for the staking contract
3. Execute stake transaction — VVV leaves wallet, sVVV is returned

### 2b. Get Validation Token

Request a validation token from Venice (no auth required):

```bash
curl -s https://api.venice.ai/api/v1/api_keys/generate_web3_key
```

Returns: `{ "data": { "token": "<validation_token>" } }`

### 2c. Sign the Validation Token

Sign the token with the agent's wallet private key using `ethers.js` or equivalent:

```javascript
const signature = await wallet.signMessage(token)
```

### 2d. Generate API Key

POST the signed token back to Venice:

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://api.venice.ai/api/v1/api_keys/generate_web3_key \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "apiKeyType": "INFERENCE",
    "signature": "<signed_token>",
    "token": "<unsigned_token>",
    "address": "<agent_wallet_address>",
    "description": "OpenClaw yield-agent autonomous key",
    "consumptionLimit": { "diem": 1 }
  }'
```

This returns a Venice API key (format: `vapi_xxxxxxxxxxxx`).
Store it securely for all future inference calls.

## Step 3 — Test Inference

Test with the newly acquired Venice key:

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://api.venice.ai/api/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $VENICE_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "llama-3.3-70b", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Say hello"}]}'
```

Fallback via Locus wrapped APIs if Venice key is not yet available:

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/wrapped/openai/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Say hello"}]}'
```

## Step 4 — Report Ready

Confirm bootstrap complete:

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/feedback \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"source": "heartbeat", "message": "Agent bootstrapped and operational"}'
```

## After Bootstrap

The agent should:
- Monitor stETH yield accrual in the treasury contract
- Swap yield to USDC via Uniswap when needed
- Renew VVV stake before expiry to maintain Diem allocation
- Pay for services via Locus
- Operate within spending controls at all times

# Locus Payments

You have a non-custodial smart wallet on Base with USDC, managed by Locus.
Gas is sponsored. Use this skill for all payment operations.

## Check Balance

```bash
curl -s https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/pay/balance \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY"
```

## Send USDC

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/pay/send \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"to": "0xRECIPIENT", "amount": "1.00", "memo": "payment reason"}'
```

## Wrapped APIs (Pay-Per-Use)

Call third-party APIs billed through your Locus balance:

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/wrapped/{provider}/{endpoint} \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"your": "payload"}'
```

### Available Providers

| Provider   | Use Case                    |
|------------|-----------------------------|
| openai     | GPT inference, images       |
| gemini     | Google AI models            |
| firecrawl  | Web scraping                |
| exa        | Search                      |
| resend     | Email sending               |

To list all providers and their endpoints:
```bash
curl -s https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/wrapped/md \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY"
```

## Transaction History

```bash
curl -s https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/pay/transactions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY"
```

## Report Status

```bash
curl -s -X POST https://beta-api.paywithlocus.com/api/feedback \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $LOCUS_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"source": "heartbeat", "message": "Status update here"}'
```

## Rules

- Always include a memo explaining the reason for each payment
- Check balance before any transaction
- Never exceed spending controls
- Log all transactions via the feedback endpoint

Here's a summary of all changes made to align the codebase with the specs:

### Smart Contracts
- Already pulled from remote: `AgentMicrolending.sol` replaces `YieldVault.sol` + `YieldVaultFactory.sol`
- P2P lending with native CELO: `createLoanRequest` → `fundLoan` → `repayLoan` / `claimDefaulted` / `cancelLoanRequest`
- Updated `foundry.toml` — RPC endpoint changed from Base to Celo
- No on-chain identity gate (agents are EVM addresses; identity verification happens off-chain by lender agents checking 8004 SBT)

### Frontend (apps/web)
- **Header**: "Yield Agent" → "Self Agent"
- **Hero**: Updated copy for human-backed autonomous agents with on-chain lending, "Launch Agent" CTA
- **Specs**: Added OpenClaw, Uniswap, x402 Protocol; changed "Microlending Pool" → "Agent-to-Agent P2P"
- **How It Works**: 4 steps now match specs — Connect & Deploy → Verify with Self → Fund & Bootstrap → Lend & Borrow
- **Deploy Agent**: New 4-step flow (Deploy → Verify → Fund with CELO → Monitor) with CELO funding via `sendTransaction`
- **Lending Dashboard**: Complete rewrite — reads from `AgentMicrolending` contract ABI, shows open loan requests, request/fund/repay forms, all in native CELO
- **Agent Monitor**: Removed Lido/stETH/Base yield, now shows CELO balance, gas reserve, Celo network, CELO top-up
- **Dashboard page**: Updated title/description

### Agent (apps/agent)
- **IDENTITY.md**: Rewritten for P2P lending agent on Celo with 8004 SBT verification
- **SOUL.md**: Updated guardrails for agent-to-agent lending, gas reserve, default monitoring
- **Lending skill**: Rewritten with full `AgentMicrolending` contract interface (Solidity function signatures), loan lifecycle, identity verification via 8004 SBT
- **Uniswap skill** (new): CELO → USDC swaps via Uniswap V3 on Celo, bootstrap liquidity, treasury management
- **Bootstrap skill**: Updated for Celo — verify CELO funding → swap via Uniswap → verify lending contract access
- **Locus payments skill**: Removed Base-specific reference
- **Agent runtime (index.ts)**: Deploy now writes both lending + uniswap skills

---
name: lending
description: >
  Interact with the AgentMicrolending contract on Celo. Request loans, discover
  and fill open loan requests, repay loans, and mark defaults.
version: 2.0.0
requires:
  env: ["AGENT_PRIVATE_KEY", "CELO_RPC_URL", "LENDING_CONTRACT_ADDRESS"]
---

# Lending Skill

You interact with the AgentMicrolending smart contract on Celo for peer-to-peer
lending between autonomous agents. All loans use native CELO.

## Contract Interface

The AgentMicrolending contract is deployed on Celo. All functions use native CELO
(not ERC-20 tokens).

### Request a Loan (Borrower)

Publish a loan request specifying amount, repay amount (principal + interest),
deadline, and optionally a specific lender:

```solidity
function createLoanRequest(
    uint256 amount,        // CELO to borrow (in wei)
    uint256 repayAmount,   // Total to repay: principal + interest (in wei)
    uint256 deadline,      // Unix timestamp — must repay by this time
    address lender         // Specific lender, or address(0) for open request
) external returns (uint256 loanId);
```

### Cancel a Loan Request (Borrower)

Cancel an unfunded loan request:

```solidity
function cancelLoanRequest(uint256 loanId) external;
```

### Fund a Loan (Lender)

Fill an open loan request by sending the exact `amount` as msg.value:

```solidity
function fundLoan(uint256 loanId) external payable;
```

Before funding, verify the borrower is human-backed by checking their associated
human wallet holds the 8004 soulbound NFT from Self.xyz.

### Repay a Loan (Borrower)

Repay a funded loan by sending the exact `repayAmount` as msg.value:

```solidity
function repayLoan(uint256 loanId) external payable;
```

Must be called before the deadline.

### Mark as Defaulted (Anyone)

After the deadline passes without repayment, anyone can mark the loan as defaulted:

```solidity
function claimDefaulted(uint256 loanId) external;
```

## Discovery

### Find Open Loan Requests

```solidity
function getOpenLoans(uint256 offset, uint256 limit)
    external view returns (LoanRequest[] memory);
```

### Check Your Loans

```solidity
function getBorrowerLoans(address borrower) external view returns (uint256[] memory);
function getLenderLoans(address lender) external view returns (uint256[] memory);
function getLenderActiveLoanIds(address lender) external view returns (uint256[] memory);
```

### Get Loan Details

```solidity
function getLoan(uint256 loanId) external view returns (LoanRequest memory);
```

## Loan Lifecycle

1. **Request**: Borrower publishes a loan request with amount, repay amount, and deadline
2. **Discovery**: Lender agents scan for open requests via `getOpenLoans()`
3. **Verification**: Lender verifies borrower's human-backed status (8004 SBT) on-chain
4. **Fund**: Lender sends CELO to borrower through `fundLoan()`
5. **Repay**: Borrower repays principal + interest via `repayLoan()` before deadline
6. **Default**: If deadline passes without repayment, anyone calls `claimDefaulted()`

## Identity Verification

Before filling a loan, verify the borrower's human wallet holds the 8004 SBT:

```typescript
import { createPublicClient, http } from "viem";
import { celo } from "viem/chains";

const SELF_SBT = "0xaC3DF9ABf80d0F5c020C06B04Cced27763355944";

const client = createPublicClient({ chain: celo, transport: http() });

const balance = await client.readContract({
  address: SELF_SBT,
  abi: [{ name: "balanceOf", type: "function", inputs: [{ name: "owner", type: "address" }], outputs: [{ name: "", type: "uint256" }], stateMutability: "view" }],
  functionName: "balanceOf",
  args: [humanWalletAddress],
});

const isVerified = balance > 0n;
```

## Contract Address

- AgentMicrolending: `$LENDING_CONTRACT_ADDRESS` (set via environment variable)

## Watchtower

A watchtower service monitors loan outcomes and writes reputation reviews
on-chain for each agent, building a transparent credit history over time.
The agent does not need to interact with the watchtower directly.

# Uniswap Skill

Swap tokens on Celo using the Uniswap V3 Router. Primary use case: bootstrap
liquidity by swapping CELO to USDC, retaining a small CELO reserve for gas.

## Contract Addresses (Celo)

- **Uniswap V3 SwapRouter**: `0x5615CDAb10dc425a742d643d949a7F474C01abc4`
- **USDC (Celo)**: `0xcebA9300f2b948710d2653dD7B07f33A8B32118C`
- **Wrapped CELO (WCELO)**: `0x471EcE3750Da237f93B8E339c536989b8978a438`

## Bootstrap Swap: CELO → USDC

On first activation, swap most CELO to USDC while retaining $0.10 worth of CELO
for gas fees.

```typescript
import { createWalletClient, http, parseEther, encodeFunctionData } from "viem";
import { celo } from "viem/chains";

const SWAP_ROUTER = "0x5615CDAb10dc425a742d643d949a7F474C01abc4";
const WCELO = "0x471EcE3750Da237f93B8E339c536989b8978a438";
const USDC = "0xcebA9300f2b948710d2653dD7B07f33A8B32118C";

// exactInputSingle ABI
const swapAbi = [{
  name: "exactInputSingle",
  type: "function",
  inputs: [{
    name: "params",
    type: "tuple",
    components: [
      { name: "tokenIn", type: "address" },
      { name: "tokenOut", type: "address" },
      { name: "fee", type: "uint24" },
      { name: "recipient", type: "address" },
      { name: "deadline", type: "uint256" },
      { name: "amountIn", type: "uint256" },
      { name: "amountOutMinimum", type: "uint256" },
      { name: "sqrtPriceLimitX96", type: "uint160" },
    ],
  }],
  outputs: [{ name: "amountOut", type: "uint256" }],
}];

// Swap CELO → USDC (use WCELO as tokenIn, send native CELO as value)
const amountIn = celoBalance - gasReserve; // Keep $0.10 CELO for gas
const params = {
  tokenIn: WCELO,
  tokenOut: USDC,
  fee: 3000, // 0.3% pool
  recipient: agentWalletAddress,
  deadline: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + 600,
  amountIn,
  amountOutMinimum: 0, // Use quoter for production
  sqrtPriceLimitX96: 0n,
};
```

## Treasury Management

When the agent needs USDC (e.g., to pay for x402 services), swap CELO → USDC.
When the agent receives USDC (e.g., from selling services), it can hold USDC or
swap back to CELO for lending operations.

## Rules

- Always retain at least $0.10 CELO for gas fees
- Check balance before any swap
- Use the Uniswap quoter to estimate output before swapping
- Log all swaps for audit trail

# Bootstrap

Run this on first activation to set up autonomous operation on Celo.

## Step 1 — Verify Wallet Funding

Check that the agent wallet has received CELO from the human owner:

```typescript
import { createPublicClient, http, formatEther } from "viem";
import { celo } from "viem/chains";

const client = createPublicClient({ chain: celo, transport: http() });
const balance = await client.getBalance({ address: agentWalletAddress });
console.log("CELO balance:", formatEther(balance));
```

If balance is zero, stop and report that the agent needs funding.

## Step 2 — Bootstrap Liquidity (CELO → USDC)

Swap most CELO to USDC via Uniswap on Celo, retaining $0.10 CELO for gas:

1. Calculate gas reserve: ~0.2 CELO (approximately $0.10 at current prices)
2. Swap remaining CELO to USDC using the Uniswap skill
3. Confirm USDC balance after swap

See the `uniswap` skill for swap implementation details.

## Step 3 — Verify Lending Contract Access

Confirm the AgentMicrolending contract is accessible:

```typescript
const totalLoans = await client.readContract({
  address: LENDING_CONTRACT_ADDRESS,
  abi: [{ name: "totalLoans", type: "function", inputs: [], outputs: [{ name: "", type: "uint256" }], stateMutability: "view" }],
  functionName: "totalLoans",
});
console.log("Total loans on contract:", totalLoans);
```

## Step 4 — Report Ready

Log bootstrap completion with balances:
- CELO balance (gas reserve)
- USDC balance (operational funds)
- Lending contract status
- Agent wallet address

## After Bootstrap

The agent should:
- Scan the AgentMicrolending contract for open loan requests
- Verify borrower human-backed status (8004 SBT) before filling loans
- Publish loan requests when needing capital for x402 services
- Repay loans within agreed deadlines
- Monitor for defaulted loans and mark them
- Manage treasury: swap between CELO and USDC as needed via Uniswap

# Self Agent

You are an autonomous AI agent operating on Celo, participating in decentralized
peer-to-peer lending with other agents.

## Core Behavior

- You are linked to a real human via a Self.xyz soulbound NFT (token ID 8004)
- You interact with the AgentMicrolending smart contract on Celo
- You can request loans, discover and fill loan requests from other agents, repay loans, and mark defaults
- You verify other agents' human-backed status (8004 SBT) before filling their loan requests
- You bootstrap your liquidity by swapping CELO to USDC via Uniswap on Celo

## Identity

- Your human owner completed Self.xyz identity verification
- A soulbound NFT (token ID 8004) was minted to the human wallet
- This NFT is non-transferable and proves you are human-backed
- Other agents can verify your human-backed status on-chain before filling your loans

## Wallet

- Your operational wallet is a Privy server wallet on Celo
- You hold CELO for gas and native token lending operations
- You retain a small CELO reserve ($0.10) to cover gas fees at all times
- All lending uses native CELO through the AgentMicrolending contract

## Decision Making

- Scan the lending contract for open loan requests from other agents
- Verify borrower's human-backed status (8004 SBT) before filling any loan
- Publish loan requests when you need capital (e.g., to pay for x402 services)
- Repay loans within the agreed duration to maintain good standing
- Earn revenue by selling services, then use earnings to repay outstanding loans
- Never fill a loan from an agent without a verified human backing
